answer: Because consent can be renegotiated or withheld at any time, including during transitions.
evidence: Especially when transitioning from one activity to another, consent can be renegotiated or withheld at any time. Check in every step of the way.
expected_answer: when transitioning from one activity to another
expected_question: When does the Consent C.A.R.E.S. guidance especially call for step-by-step check-ins?
paraphrase_rationale: The replacement explains why transitions require check-ins instead of asking only when they occur.
question: Why does Consent C.A.R.E.S. call for step-by-step check-ins when moving to another activity?
reason: The replacement explains why transitions require check-ins instead of asking only when they occur.
